title: Understanding the Pythagorean Theorem
text: The Pythagorean Theorem is used to find the length of a side in a right triangle. It states that in a right triangle, the square of the hypotenuse (the side opposite the right angle) is equal to the sum of the squares of the other two sides.

title: Identifying the Right Triangle
text: To use the Pythagorean Theorem, you need to identify the right triangle. In this problem, we are given a triangular garden. Check if the given sides can form a right triangle.

title: Using the Pythagorean Theorem
text: If the triangle is a right triangle, use the formula: $$a^2+b^2=c^2$$, where c is the hypotenuse. Identify which side is the hypotenuse and which are the other two sides.

title: Calculating the Third Side
text: If the triangle is not a right triangle, use the perimeter to find the third side. The perimeter is the sum of all sides of the triangle.

title: Finding the Third Side Using Perimeter
text: The perimeter of the triangle is $$24$$ feet. Two sides are $$4$$ feet and $$9$$ feet. Use the equation: $$4+9+x=24$$ to find the third side.
